items.content: Kwame Asante: Final-year economics student at the University of Ghana and co-founder of an agri-tech startup. He is passionate about African development economics and aims to connect smallholder cocoa farmers to premium buyers.
University of Ghana: Kwame Asante's current institution where he is pursuing his final year in economics. This is a key academic environment shaping his future career and entrepreneurial endeavors.

Agri-tech Startup: Kwame Asante is a co-founder of an agri-tech startup focused on mobile platforms for cocoa farmers. This venture represents his entrepreneurial drive and commitment to improving agricultural value chains.
Accra, Ghana: Kwame Asante's home city and the location of the University of Ghana. It serves as the base for his studies, entrepreneurial activities, and personal life.
African Development Economics: A core passion for Kwame Asante, influencing his academic pursuits and the focus of his agri-tech startup. He actively writes about this topic in his newsletter.

Economics Newsletter: A weekly publication authored by Kwame Asante, reaching 3,000 subscribers. This platform disseminates his thoughts on development economics and highlights his communication skills.
Rhodes Scholarship: A significant aspiration for Kwame Asante, aiming for postgraduate studies at Oxford University. This goal reflects his ambition and commitment to academic excellence.
Baptist Church: Kwame Asante serves as a deacon in his local Baptist church. This indicates his religious involvement and community commitment.
